title: PLC mode changes during active OT incidents

value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for TSA SD compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot and sourcetypes rockwell:audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=ot`.
visualization: Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value. Key fields: `controller`, `incident_id`, `uc_tag`, `user`.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Incidents during planned exercises, controlled tabletop drills, or vendor maintenance windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype=rockwell:audit earliest=-30d message="*Mode*Change*"
| eval uc_tag="22.16.17"
| lookup active_ir_incidents.csv start_time end_time OUTPUT incident_id
| where isnotnull(incident_id)
| stats count by incident_id, controller, user
```

Understanding this SPL

**PLC mode changes during active OT incidents (TSA containment)** — Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "PLC mode changes during active OT incidents".

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: rockwell:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype=rockwell:audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(incident_id)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by incident_id, controller, user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value
grandmaExplanation: We support pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "PLC mode changes during active OT incidents". That lines up with pipeline security expectations and helps the team show its work to regulators and leadership.
